chapter_summary: Chapter 3 is one of the most loved chapters in all of Proverbs. It tells us to trust God completely — not to think we know better than Him. When we trust God with everything we do, He shows us the right path. This chapter also tells us to be kind and honest, to give to others generously, and to remember that God teaches us because He loves us, just like a good father.
main_lesson: Trust God with everything. He will show you the right way to go.
memory_verse: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
parent_guide: Proverbs 3 is rich with practical and theological depth. Key teaching moments: (1) Verse 5-6 — the cornerstone of the chapter and one of the most memorised verses in Scripture. Help children understand that 'leaning on our own understanding' means assuming we always know best. (2) Verse 3 — kindness and truth together make a whole person. (3) Verse 12 — God's discipline is an expression of love. If your child has ever been corrected, use this to help them understand why. (4) Verse 9-10 — honouring God with our 'firstfruits' is a giving principle even young children can practise (e.g., giving first from their pocket money).
application_for_children: Practice saying 'God, I trust You' whenever something feels scary or confusing this week. When you must make a choice, ask: 'What would God want me to do here?'

verses.kjv: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:
verses.little_bible: My child, do not forget what I have taught you. Keep God's rules in your heart.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't forget God's rules. Keep them in your heart.
verses.meaning: God wants us to hold His words in our hearts, not just our heads — to love His ways and live by them.
verses.memory_phrase: I keep God's rules in my heart.
verses.prayer: God, help me to remember Your words and not forget them.
verses.discussion_question: What is one of God's rules that you love?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to keep God's words in our hearts, not just our heads?
verses.do_it_today: Say one of God's commands from memory before bedtime tonight.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gently placing glowing words into their heart like putting letters in a treasure box

verses.kjv: For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.
verses.little_bible: Keeping God's words will add good days, long life, and peace to you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's ways give you a good and peaceful life.
verses.meaning: Living by God's wisdom brings long-lasting blessing, peace, and a life well-lived — not because we earn it, but because God's ways are good for us.
verses.memory_phrase: God's ways bring peace and life.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that following Your ways brings peace and goodness into my life.
verses.discussion_question: What does peace feel like? Can you think of a time you felt really peaceful?
verses.family_discussion: How has following God's ways brought peace to your family?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that brings peace to your home — something kind or calming.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sleeping peacefully, surrounded by warm golden light, a gentle smile on their face

verses.kjv: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:
verses.little_bible: Always be kind and truthful. Wear kindness and honesty like a necklace — carry them everywhere.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Be kind and tell the truth everywhere you go.
verses.meaning: God wants kindness and truth to be part of who we are — visible in how we treat others every single day.
verses.memory_phrase: I am kind and honest.
verses.prayer: God, help me be kind and tell the truth, even when it is hard.
verses.discussion_question: Can you be kind but tell a lie at the same time? Why not?
verses.family_discussion: Name one way you can show kindness and truth to someone today.
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing AND say something true and helpful to someone today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child wearing a glowing necklace with the words 'Kind' and 'True' shining on it

verses.kjv: So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
verses.little_bible: When you are kind and true, both God and other people will love and respect you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Being kind and honest makes God and people happy.
verses.meaning: A life marked by kindness and truth earns favour — not just with people, but with God Himself. This is what it looks like to be well-loved.
verses.memory_phrase: Kindness and truth make me well-loved.
verses.prayer: God, let kindness and truth make me someone You and others love to be around.
verses.discussion_question: Why do people trust and like someone who is kind and honest?
verses.family_discussion: Who do you know that has a great reputation because of their kindness and honesty?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for a neighbour or classmate today — let kindness build your reputation.
verses.illustration_prompt: A smiling child in the middle with God's light above and a group of happy friends around them

verses.kjv: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
verses.little_bible: Trust God with your whole heart. Do not think you always know best — let God lead you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Trust God with all your heart. Let God lead you.
verses.meaning: True trust means handing all our plans and worries to God, even when we do not understand what He is doing.
verses.memory_phrase: I trust God with all my heart.
verses.prayer: God, I trust You with everything — my day, my feelings, my life.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to trust someone completely?
verses.family_discussion: Is there something confusing or scary you can give to God right now?
verses.do_it_today: Hold your hands out, palms up, and give something you are worried about to God in prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with open hands looking up, with a beam of warm light from heaven resting on them peacefully

verses.kjv: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.
verses.little_bible: In everything you do, think about God and ask Him. He will show you the right way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Ask God for help in everything. He will show you the way.
verses.meaning: When we involve God in every part of our day — not just church or prayer time — He guides our feet on the right path.
verses.memory_phrase: God directs my path.
verses.prayer: God, be part of everything I do today. Show me the right way.
verses.discussion_question: What would it look like to include God in your whole day, not just at bedtime?
verses.family_discussion: How can you include God in ordinary moments — breakfast, school, play?
verses.do_it_today: Before each new activity today, whisper: 'God, lead me in this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking along a glowing golden path through a beautiful garden, with God's light shining ahead

verses.kjv: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil.
verses.little_bible: Don't think you know everything on your own. Respect God and turn away from what is wrong.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't think you know best. Respect God and do what is right.
verses.meaning: Pride in our own wisdom is dangerous. True wisdom begins with respecting God and turning away from evil.
verses.memory_phrase: I respect God and turn from wrong.
verses.prayer: God, keep me humble. Help me always respect You and turn away from what is wrong.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when someone always thinks they know best and never listens?
verses.family_discussion: How does respecting God keep us humble and on the right path?
verses.do_it_today: When you feel like you know best today, pause and ask: 'God, what do You think?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child pausing thoughtfully, with a gentle glow showing they are listening to God rather than charging ahead

verses.kjv: It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.
verses.little_bible: Living God's way is like the best food for your body — it makes you healthy and strong inside.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's ways make you healthy and strong.
verses.meaning: God's wisdom is not just good for the soul — it brings wholeness to the whole person. Following God's ways nourishes us completely.
verses.memory_phrase: God's ways make me healthy and strong.
verses.prayer: God, let Your wisdom make me strong in every way — body, mind, and heart.
verses.discussion_question: What foods make you strong and healthy? How is God's wisdom like that for your heart?
verses.family_discussion: How does following God's ways help us be healthy — not just in our bodies but in our hearts and minds?
verses.do_it_today: Do something healthy for your body today AND read one verse for your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: A strong, joyful child with golden light running through them like good healthy nourishment

verses.kjv: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:
verses.little_bible: Honour God with the good things you have. Give Him the first and best of what you receive.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Give your best to God first.
verses.meaning: Giving to God first — before spending on ourselves — shows that we trust Him and love Him more than our things.
verses.memory_phrase: I give God my best first.
verses.prayer: God, help me to give to You first and to be generous like You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to give God your 'first and best'?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, how can we honour God with what we have?
verses.do_it_today: If you have pocket money or a treat, think about giving some of it to God or someone in need first.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child joyfully placing the first fruits from a basket on a bright altar, with a smile on their face

verses.kjv: So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.
verses.little_bible: When you give to God generously, He will fill your life with more than enough.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When you give to God, He gives you more than enough.
verses.meaning: God loves a generous giver and promises to bless those who honour Him with their resources. His provision overflows to those who give first.
verses.memory_phrase: When I give, God fills me up.
verses.prayer: God, help me give generously and trust You to fill my life with what I need.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever shared something and found you still had enough?
verses.family_discussion: Tell a story of a time God provided more than enough for your family.
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today — and trust God to provide what you need.
verses.illustration_prompt: Overflowing baskets of fruit and bread, a child laughing joyfully in the middle of a bountiful harvest

verses.kjv: My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD; neither be weary of his correction:
verses.little_bible: My child, do not hate it when God corrects you. Do not give up when He teaches you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't be upset when God corrects you. He loves you.
verses.meaning: God's correction is not punishment to fear or reject — it is a sign of His love. Being teachable is a mark of wisdom.
verses.memory_phrase: God's correction is a sign of love.
verses.prayer: God, help me be willing to be corrected and taught, even when it is hard.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel when someone corrects you? Is it easy or hard?
verses.family_discussion: Why is being willing to be corrected a sign of wisdom?
verses.do_it_today: If someone corrects you today, try to say 'thank you' and mean it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a thoughtful, open expression listening to a gentle, loving adult who is guiding them

verses.kjv: For whom the LORD loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.
verses.little_bible: God teaches us and corrects us because He loves us — just like a good father who cares about his child.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When God corrects us, it is because He loves us.
verses.meaning: Being corrected by God is a sign of His love — He is training us to grow up wise and good, just as a loving parent does.
verses.memory_phrase: God corrects me because He loves me.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for loving me enough to teach me when I go wrong.
verses.discussion_question: Why does a good parent sometimes tell their child off?
verses.family_discussion: How is being corrected by someone who loves you different from being told off by someone who does not care?
verses.do_it_today: Thank a parent or teacher today for correcting you — tell them it shows they care.
verses.illustration_prompt: A loving father kneeling down, gently speaking to a child who is listening with a soft, open face

verses.kjv: Happy is the man that findeth wisdom, and the man that getteth understanding.
verses.little_bible: The person who finds wisdom is so happy! Getting understanding is wonderful.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Finding wisdom makes you very happy.
verses.meaning: Wisdom brings deep joy and happiness — not just to know the right things, but to live well by them.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom makes me happy.
verses.prayer: God, let me find wisdom and be truly happy in You.
verses.discussion_question: What makes you the most happy? Is wisdom on your list?
verses.family_discussion: Why is wisdom a better source of happiness than toys or fun things?
verses.do_it_today: Share one wise thing you have learned recently that made you feel good.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child jumping for joy, arms wide open, with a glowing Bible or scroll nearby

verses.kjv: For the merchandise of it is better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom is worth more than silver or gold. Getting wisdom is better than getting rich.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom is worth more than all the gold in the world.
verses.meaning: No amount of money can buy what wisdom gives. Wisdom leads to a life of meaning, goodness, and true blessing.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is worth more than gold.
verses.prayer: God, help me want wisdom more than I want to be rich.
verses.discussion_question: If you had to choose between a bag of gold coins and being very wise, which would you pick? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Can money buy wisdom? What can it not buy?
verses.do_it_today: Make a list of things wisdom gives you that money cannot buy.
verses.illustration_prompt: A scale with gold coins on one side and a glowing book of wisdom on the other — the wisdom side tips down because it is heavier

verses.kjv: She is more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom is more precious than rubies — nothing you could ever want can compare to it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom is more precious than any jewel.
verses.meaning: Wisdom outshines every material desire. There is nothing we could wish for that is more valuable than wisdom from God.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is the most precious thing.
verses.prayer: God, help me treasure wisdom above all things.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most precious thing you own? Is wisdom more precious than that?
verses.family_discussion: Why does God say wisdom is worth more than anything we could desire?
verses.do_it_today: Find a gem or a jewel (even a picture of one) and hold it, then remember wisdom is even more precious.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing ruby in one hand, but the child's eyes looking with even more wonder at a bright, radiant scroll in the other hand

verses.kjv: Length of days is in her right hand; and in her left hand riches and honour.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom gives you a long, good life in one hand, and blessings and honour in the other.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom gives you a good long life and blessings.
verses.meaning: Wisdom carries two great gifts: a full, long life and the honour and abundance that come from living well.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom gives me a good and full life.
verses.prayer: God, let wisdom lead me to a life that is full, good, and honouring to You.
verses.discussion_question: What would a really good, long life look like to you?
verses.family_discussion: How does wisdom help us live lives that are both long and meaningful?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone old and wise in your community — pray for them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wise figure holding glowing gifts in both hands — a sunrise on the right and shining stars on the left

verses.kjv: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom's ways are lovely and pleasant. Every path wisdom leads to is full of peace.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom's paths are pleasant and full of peace.
verses.meaning: Following wisdom does not lead to hardship — it leads to a life of beauty, peace, and delight. God's ways are good ways.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom's paths are peaceful.
verses.prayer: God, lead me on wisdom's pleasant, peaceful paths.
verses.discussion_question: What does a peaceful path feel like? Can you imagine a calm, happy journey?
verses.family_discussion: How have you seen God's ways be pleasant and peaceful in real life?
verses.do_it_today: Take a peaceful walk today and thank God for the good paths He leads us on.
verses.illustration_prompt: A winding path through a beautiful flower-filled meadow with sunlight and peace — a child walking calmly and joyfully

verses.kjv: She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her: and happy is every one that retaineth her.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom is like a tree of life — hold onto her and be happy! Everyone who keeps wisdom is truly blessed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hold onto wisdom. It gives you life and happiness.
verses.meaning: Wisdom is life-giving like a beautiful tree. Those who hold onto it are sustained and made happy — it is one of God's great gifts.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is my tree of life.
verses.prayer: God, help me hold onto wisdom all my days and find life in it.
verses.discussion_question: How is wisdom like a tree that gives you fruit and shade?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like to 'hold onto' wisdom — to not let it go?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a tree and write 'wisdom' on it — then write on the branches things wisdom gives you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A magnificent glowing tree with golden leaves, a happy child sitting in its branches, safe and delighted

verses.kjv: The LORD by wisdom hath founded the earth; by understanding hath he established the heavens.
verses.little_bible: God used wisdom to make the whole earth! He used understanding to put all the heavens in their place.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God made the whole world with wisdom.
verses.meaning: The very universe was designed with wisdom. This shows us that wisdom is not just for people — it is God's own way of working.
verses.memory_phrase: God made the world with wisdom.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for creating this beautiful world with Your wisdom.
verses.discussion_question: Look outside — what is one amazing thing God made? How much wisdom did He need to make it?
verses.family_discussion: How does seeing God's wisdom in creation make you want to seek wisdom more?
verses.do_it_today: Go outside and find one amazing thing in creation — thank God for the wisdom He used to make it.
verses.illustration_prompt: The earth and stars and sky viewed from a hilltop, a child looking up in wonder at the vast, beautiful creation

verses.kjv: By his knowledge the depths are broken up, and the clouds drop down the dew.
verses.little_bible: God's knowledge fills the oceans deep and brings the gentle dew down from the clouds.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's knowledge fills the deep ocean and sends the rain.
verses.meaning: God's wisdom and knowledge sustain all of nature — from the deepest ocean to the smallest dewdrop. His knowledge is behind everything that lives.
verses.memory_phrase: God's knowledge is in all of creation.
verses.prayer: God, Your knowledge is so great. Thank You for caring for every part of Your world.
verses.discussion_question: Have you seen dew on the grass in the morning? Who puts it there?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing that God made and knows all of nature help us trust Him with our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Find a droplet of water — in the morning dew, a glass, or a raindrop — and thank God for His amazing knowledge.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child in the early morning, eyes wide, touching a dewdrop on a leaf, with sunlight breaking through trees

verses.kjv: My son, let not them depart from thine eyes: keep sound wisdom and discretion:
verses.little_bible: My child, do not let wisdom and good thinking leave your sight. Keep them close.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep wisdom close. Don't let it go.
verses.meaning: We must keep wisdom always in view — not just learn it once and forget it, but actively keep it in front of us every day.
verses.memory_phrase: I keep wisdom close every day.
verses.prayer: God, help me keep wisdom in front of my eyes and not forget it.
verses.discussion_question: How do you keep important things from getting lost?
verses.family_discussion: What habits help your family keep God's wisdom always in view?
verses.do_it_today: Put a wise verse somewhere you will see it today — on the mirror, the fridge, or your desk.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a small glowing scroll or card in their hand that they are looking at with focused, attentive eyes

verses.kjv: So shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace to thy neck.
verses.little_bible: Wisdom will be life to your soul and like a beautiful ornament worn around your neck.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom is life for your soul and makes you beautiful.
verses.meaning: Wisdom gives the soul what it needs to live, and it makes us beautiful in God's eyes — adorning our character with grace.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom is life for my soul.
verses.prayer: God, let Your wisdom fill my soul with life and grace.
verses.discussion_question: What makes someone truly beautiful on the inside?
verses.family_discussion: How does wisdom make a person beautiful in a way that lasts?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to say something wise and kind today — let wisdom be your ornament.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a gentle glow around them, wearing a simple necklace that shines — inner beauty made visible

verses.kjv: Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.
verses.little_bible: When you have wisdom, you will walk safely. Your feet will not trip or stumble.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom helps you walk safely without tripping.
verses.meaning: Living wisely gives us confident, safe steps through life. We will not be thrown by surprises when wisdom guides us.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom keeps my feet safe.
verses.prayer: God, let wisdom steady my feet and keep me from stumbling.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you are not looking where you are going?
verses.family_discussion: How does wisdom help us see where we are stepping in life?
verses.do_it_today: As you walk somewhere today, think about God guiding your steps — both literally and in your choices.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking confidently on a bright, clear path, feet steady, eyes forward, with golden light ahead

verses.kjv: When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.
verses.little_bible: When you go to bed, you will not be afraid. You will lie down and sleep sweetly.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's wisdom gives you sweet, peaceful sleep.
verses.meaning: Wisdom brings inner peace that carries us through the night. Those who trust God do not need to lie awake in fear.
verses.memory_phrase: God gives me sweet, peaceful sleep.
verses.prayer: God, take away my fears at night and give me sweet sleep.
verses.discussion_question: What sometimes makes you feel afraid at night? How can thinking about God help?
verses.family_discussion: How does trusting God in the daytime help us sleep peacefully at night?
verses.do_it_today: At bedtime tonight, say a short prayer giving your fears to God before you sleep.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sleeping peacefully in a cosy bed, soft golden light from a window, completely safe and at rest

verses.kjv: Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.
verses.little_bible: Do not be suddenly frightened, and do not be scared when bad things happen to those who do wrong.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Do not be suddenly afraid. God is with you.
verses.meaning: Wisdom means we do not need to be thrown by sudden alarming events or the chaos around us — God is our steady foundation.
verses.memory_phrase: God steadies me when I am scared.
verses.prayer: God, when something scary happens suddenly, help me remember You are right there with me.
verses.discussion_question: When something scary happens suddenly, what is the first thing you should do?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing God is with us help when something unexpected and scary happens?
verses.do_it_today: Memorise this: 'God is with me. I will not be afraid.' Say it if something scares you today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking calm and steady during a storm, with God's hand visibly over them like a shelter

verses.kjv: For the LORD shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.
verses.little_bible: God is your confidence — He will keep your foot from being caught or trapped.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God keeps your foot from being caught. Trust Him.
verses.meaning: Our confidence comes from God, not from ourselves. He protects us from traps and dangers we cannot even see.
verses.memory_phrase: God is my confidence.
verses.prayer: God, be my confidence today. Keep my feet from every trap.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be confident? Where does your confidence come from?
verses.family_discussion: How is trusting in God better than trusting only in yourself?
verses.do_it_today: When you feel unsure today, say: 'God is my confidence. He will keep me safe.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking boldly across a tricky path with God's light guiding each step safely

verses.kjv: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it.
verses.little_bible: When you have the chance to do something good for someone, do it! Don't hold back.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When you can do good, do it right away.
verses.meaning: Doing good should be immediate when we have the ability. Holding back good that we could give is not God's way.
verses.memory_phrase: When I can do good, I do it.
verses.prayer: God, help me not hold back from doing good when I have the chance.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time when you could do something kind but waited too long?
verses.family_discussion: Who do you know right now who needs something good that your family could give?
verses.do_it_today: Do one good thing for someone today that you have been putting off.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child happily handing a gift to a smiling neighbour, with sunshine and flowers in the background

verses.kjv: Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.
verses.little_bible: Don't tell your neighbour 'Come back tomorrow' when you have what they need right now.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Help people now when you can, not tomorrow.
verses.meaning: Putting off helping when we have the means to help is a form of selfishness. God calls us to be immediately generous.
verses.memory_phrase: I help people now, not later.
verses.prayer: God, make me quick to help when someone needs me.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it better to help someone right away instead of saying 'later'?
verses.family_discussion: How can our family practise being generous right away when someone has a need?
verses.do_it_today: Find someone who needs help today and help them right away — don't put it off.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child immediately reaching out to help a friend who dropped something, not walking past

verses.kjv: Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.
verses.little_bible: Don't plan anything wrong against your neighbour who lives near you and trusts you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't plan anything unkind against your neighbour.
verses.meaning: Planning harm against those who trust us is a deep betrayal. God calls us to honour the trust our neighbours place in us.
verses.memory_phrase: I am safe to my neighbours.
verses.prayer: God, help me be a good neighbour — someone people can trust and feel safe around.
verses.discussion_question: What makes a good neighbour? What makes a bad one?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family be a blessing and a safe presence to those who live near you?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing for a neighbour today — wave, say hello, or do something helpful.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two neighbours smiling and waving over a fence in a sunny, friendly street

verses.kjv: Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.
verses.little_bible: Don't pick a fight with someone for no reason — especially if they have not hurt you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't argue or fight for no reason.
verses.meaning: Quarrelling without cause is foolish and harmful. Wise people choose peace and do not stir up conflict unnecessarily.
verses.memory_phrase: I choose peace, not fighting.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose peace and not start arguments for no reason.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever argued about something that did not really matter?
verses.family_discussion: How do we know when something is worth disagreeing about and when it is not?
verses.do_it_today: If someone annoys you today, count to five and ask: 'Is this worth a fight?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children pausing and smiling at each other instead of arguing, a rainbow in the peaceful sky above them

verses.kjv: Envy thou not the oppressor, and choose none of his ways.
verses.little_bible: Don't wish you were like someone who is mean and powerful. Don't copy their ways.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't copy people who are mean and unkind.
verses.meaning: It is tempting to envy people who seem to get ahead by being cruel or unjust. But their way leads to ruin — we must not imitate them.
verses.memory_phrase: I don't copy unkind ways.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be jealous of those who do wrong, even if they seem powerful.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like someone who was mean still got all the good things? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Why does God say not to envy people who seem to win by being unkind?
verses.do_it_today: If you feel envious of someone today, pray for them instead of wishing you were like them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child choosing a bright path while looking calmly away from a powerful but shadowy figure on a darker road

verses.kjv: For the froward is abomination to the LORD: but his secret is with the righteous.
verses.little_bible: God dislikes people who are twisted and dishonest. But He shares His secrets with those who do right.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God shares His secrets with those who do what is right.
verses.meaning: Dishonesty and twisted ways are deeply displeasing to God. But those who walk uprightly are brought into His confidence — He shares His heart with them.
verses.memory_phrase: God shares His heart with the righteous.
verses.prayer: God, help me live rightly so I can be close to You and know Your heart.
verses.discussion_question: Who are the people you share your biggest secrets with? Why do you trust them?
verses.family_discussion: How does living rightly bring us closer to God and into His confidence?
verses.do_it_today: Spend five minutes quietly with God today — listen for what He might want to share with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting quietly with God, leaning in close, as if sharing a wonderful secret in warm golden light

verses.kjv: The curse of the LORD is in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.
verses.little_bible: God's blessing is on the home of those who do right, but not on those who keep doing wrong.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God blesses the homes of those who do what is right.
verses.meaning: The choices made in a family have a spiritual dimension. A home where God is honoured becomes a place of blessing.
verses.memory_phrase: God blesses our home when we do right.
verses.prayer: God, let Your blessing be on our home because we choose to honour You.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of things fill your home with blessing?
verses.family_discussion: As a family, what can we do to make sure our home is a place where God's blessing rests?
verses.do_it_today: As a family, say a prayer together asking God to bless your home.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm, glowing family home with soft light in every window, surrounded by a beautiful peaceful garden

verses.kjv: Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly.
verses.little_bible: God turns away from proud people who mock others, but He gives grace and kindness to those who are humble.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God gives His kindness to humble people.
verses.meaning: Pride pushes God away, but humility draws His grace close. This is one of the most important principles in all of Scripture.
verses.memory_phrase: God gives grace to the humble.
verses.prayer: God, make me humble so that I can receive Your grace.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between being proud and being humble?
verses.family_discussion: Why does God give His grace to humble people — what is it about humility that God loves?
verses.do_it_today: Find one thing to be humble about today — give credit to God or another person instead of yourself.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child kneeling gently with hands open, and a soft shower of golden light — grace — raining down on them

verses.kjv: The wise shall inherit glory: but shame shall be the promotion of fools.
verses.little_bible: Those who are wise will receive glory and honour, but foolish people will only find shame.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wise choices lead to honour. Foolish choices lead to shame.
verses.meaning: The end of wisdom is glory and honour; the end of foolishness is shame. Every choice today shapes the kind of person we become.
verses.memory_phrase: Wise choices lead to honour.
verses.prayer: God, help me make wise choices today so my life brings You glory.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a choice that seemed fun at first but led to a not-so-good ending?
verses.family_discussion: How do the small wise choices we make today build up to a life of honour?
verses.do_it_today: Make one wise choice today that you are proud of — something that honours God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child in the glow of a golden crown of honour, looking joyful and peaceful — wisdom's reward visible
